title: Inventory Player Modules
summary: Current-state inventory of source-only metadata for the eight committed src/player/ TypeScript modules that model the vanilla DOOM player surface: spawn/state/psprite plumbing, ticcmd-driven horizontal and vertical movement, weapon ownership and ammo, the weapon sprite state machine, hitscan and projectile attack actions, pickup application, and powerup colormap/palette ticking, without embedding proprietary WAD bytes.
evidence_method: inventory lane local repository audit using git ls-files-compatible Bun.Glob scans, sha256sum-equivalent SHA-256 fingerprints, src/player/, test/player/, reference/manifests/vanilla-limit-summary.json, reference/manifests/source-catalog.json Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 anchors, and package.json Bun script context.

modules.role: Implements hitscan weapon psprite actions (fist/chainsaw/pistol/shotgun/SSG/chaingun) plus the bullet-slope autoaim cascade and gunshot helpers via injected aim and line-attack callbacks.
modules.vanilla_concept_modeled: Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 p_pspr.c A_Punch, A_Saw, A_FirePistol, A_FireShotgun, A_FireShotgun2, A_FireCGun and p_map.c P_BulletSlope/P_GunShot hitscan attack behavior.

modules.role: Applies ticcmd thrust to player momentum, runs view-height bob/landing smoothing, and tics walk-state transitions when the player is on the ground.
modules.vanilla_concept_modeled: Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 p_user.c P_Thrust, P_CalcHeight, and P_MovePlayer ticcmd application behavior.

modules.role: Applies sphere/health/armor/keycard/powerup/ammo/weapon pickup effects, raises pickup messages, and routes touch-special interactions through an injected context.
modules.vanilla_concept_modeled: Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 p_inter.c P_GiveBody, P_GiveArmor, P_GiveCard, P_GivePower, and P_TouchSpecialThing pickup behavior.

modules.role: Defines the Player record (mobj, ticcmd, weapons, ammo, cards, powers, pending psprites), psprite slot definitions, weapon info table, and spawn/reborn/psprite-setup helpers.
modules.vanilla_concept_modeled: Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 d_player.h player_t struct, p_pspr.c P_SetPsprite/P_BringUpWeapon/P_SetupPsprites, and g_game.c G_PlayerReborn behavior.

modules.role: Tics powerup countdown timers, computes the active fixed colormap (invulnerability/infrared), and computes the per-tic palette index for damage/bonus/radiation flashes.
modules.vanilla_concept_modeled: Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 p_user.c P_PlayerInSpecialSector powerup tic and st_stuff.c ST_doPaletteStuff palette/colormap selection behavior.

modules.role: Implements rocket/plasma/BFG psprite actions, projectile spawn-with-aim, missile-spawn collision check, missile explosion, and BFG spray ray traversal via injected callbacks.
modules.vanilla_concept_modeled: Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 p_pspr.c A_FireMissile/A_FirePlasma/A_FireBFG/A_BFGSpray and p_mobj.c P_SpawnPlayerMissile/P_CheckMissileSpawn/P_ExplodeMissile behavior.

modules.role: Drives the weapon sprite state machine (ready/lower/raise/refire/checkreload/gunflash/light) including fire dispatch, weapon switching, and ticcmd-driven attack triggering.
modules.vanilla_concept_modeled: Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 p_pspr.c A_WeaponReady, A_Lower, A_Raise, A_ReFire, A_CheckReload, A_GunFlash, A_Light0/1/2 and P_FireWeapon/P_DropWeapon weapon state behavior.

modules.role: Manages weapon ownership, ammo accounting, P_GiveWeapon/P_GiveAmmo/P_GiveBackpack semantics, and P_CheckAmmo weapon-down switch behavior.
modules.vanilla_concept_modeled: Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 p_inter.c P_GiveAmmo, P_GiveWeapon, P_GiveBackpack and p_pspr.c P_CheckAmmo behavior.

test_coverage.notes: test/player/ covers hitscan attack flow, player movement bob and view height, pickup application, player spawn/reborn and psprite setup, powerup tic and palette/colormap selection, projectile spawning and BFG spray, weapon ownership and ammo accounting, and weapon sprite state machine transitions.

reference_manifest_status.notes: Manifest alignment records the vanilla MAXPLAYERS limit and the canonical Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 source files that the player modules model; this inventory does not embed WAD bytes or proprietary game data.

player_boundary_status.notes: src/player/ owns the player record, ticcmd-driven movement, weapon ownership and ammo, weapon sprite state machine, hitscan and projectile attack actions, pickup application, and powerup palette/colormap ticking; all attack and pickup modules accept world-side callbacks (line attack, aim, spawn mobj, try-move, damage, sound) through injected contexts so they can be unit-tested without the full collision and audio subsystems.

implications: src/player/playerSpawn.ts is the broadest export surface in the player lane (six const enums, three interfaces, the WEAPON_INFO table, and the canonical Player record) and has the highest reuse footprint (13 src/ importers and 14 test importers); any reshape of the Player struct or weapon/ammo enums is a cross-lane breaking change that the gameplay, ai, save, ui, audio, and renderer lanes must absorb together.

Hitscan and projectile attack modules accept world-side callbacks (P_LineAttack, P_AimLineAttack, P_SpawnMobj, P_TryMove, P_DamageMobj, P_StartSound) through injected HitscanContext and ProjectileContext records, so the launch and gameplay lanes must wire those callbacks at startup before src/player/hitscan.ts and src/player/projectiles.ts can fire; Phase 07 follow-ups 07-025 through 07-027 cover that wiring.

Weapon state machine (src/player/weaponStates.ts) and pickup/ammo modules (src/player/pickups.ts, src/player/weapons.ts) embed DEH_-prefixed constants (DEH_MAX_HEALTH, DEH_MAX_ARMOR, DEH_SOULSPHERE_HEALTH, DEH_MEGASPHERE_HEALTH, DEH_GREEN_ARMOR_CLASS, DEH_BLUE_ARMOR_CLASS) that mirror Dehacked-tunable vanilla defaults; downstream parity tests must keep those values pinned to the Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 defaults until a Dehacked subsystem is introduced.

src/player/movement.ts is the only player module that directly imports from src/world/zMovement.ts, while all other player modules import from src/world/mobj.ts and src/world/thinkers.ts; this confirms the player lane treats vertical movement as a world subsystem dependency rather than an internal player concern.

The MAXPLAYERS=4 vanilla limit pins the size of the player array referenced by NUMPSPRITES psprite slots and per-player ammo/weapons/cards arrays in src/player/playerSpawn.ts; Phase 07 follow-ups must keep the Player record fixed-size and avoid introducing dynamic player-count growth.
